skill1.kitLine: Activates at the start of battle. Affects self. Forcefully uses Skill 2.
skill1.where: skill2 blocks (both passive-trigger blocks fire at t0 as the force-cast)
skill1.note: S1a is not a block in the skill1 array (skill1: []); it is implemented as the t0 passive activation of both skill2 blocks. Note explicitly states this is IMPLEMENTED, hence not in unmodeled.

skill1.kitLine: Activates when an ally or self destroys an enemy's part. Affects self. Sustained Damage ▲ 5.1% for 30 sec.
skill1.note: Part-destroy trigger can never fire on the partless scope-lock boss — genuinely-skippable class.

skill1.kitLine: Activates when an ally or self destroys an enemy's part. Affects self if in Dancing Flower status. Dancing Flower Duration ▲ 10.02 sec.
skill1.note: Part-destroy trigger inert vs partless boss — genuinely-skippable class.

skill1.kitLine: Activates when an ally or self destroys an enemy's part. Affects all enemies who are in Sakura Petals status. Sakura Petals Duration ▲ 10.02 sec.
skill1.note: Part-destroy trigger inert vs partless boss — genuinely-skippable class.

skill2.kitLine: Affects self. Dancing Flower: Attack Damage ▲ 15.64% for 15 sec.
skill2.note: Time-averaged: 15.64×15/180 = 1.30 permanent (engine passive buffs ignore durationSec / alwaysOn). Approximation ⚑3; rises to ~15.64 permanent if S2 re-cast is real (⚑2).

skill2.kitLine: Affects the enemy with the highest final ATK. Sakura Petals: Deals 256% of final ATK as sustained damage every 1 sec for 15 sec.
skill2.where: skill2[1] — passive/enemy dot atkPct 256, durationSec 15, intervalSec 1, flavor sustained
skill2.note: Kit-literal single 15s window at t=0. Target 'enemy' vs single boss is equivalent to 'highest final ATK enemy'. No re-cast/extension channel fires vs partless boss. ⚑2 flags whether periodic re-cast exists.

burst.kitLine: Affects random enemies. Deals 457.14% of final ATK as damage. Attacks sequentially 10 times.
burst.where: burst[0] — burstCast/enemy, 10× flatDamage atkPct 457.14 flavor sequential
burst.note: 10 instances kept (not consolidated to 4571.4) so skillGauge fires per hit. Same-frame landing vs real ~1-2s spread is negligible. Sequential flavor tag lets teammates' Sequential Damage ▲ buffs scale it (⚑5).

burst.kitLine: Affects the same targets. Deals 35.16% of final ATK as sustained damage every 1 sec, stacks up to 10 times and lasts for 10 sec.
burst.where: burst[1] — burstCast/enemy dot atkPct 351.6, durationSec 10, intervalSec 1, flavor sustained
burst.note: 351.6 = 35.16×10 stacks, assumes all 10 stacks apply per cast (hit-applied full stacks from tick 1). dur 10 < burst interval 40s → no overlap. ⚑4 flags alternative per-second ramp reading (×0.55 of shipped).

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: force-cast ONCE at battle start (S1) — Sakura Petals 256%/s + Dancing Flower modeled as a single 15s window (t=0–15); the part-destroy duration extensions never fire vs the partless boss, so she is a plain AR after ~t=15 until her bursts ⚑
documentedConfounds.why: If S2 has a periodic re-cast (no cooldown in datamine, but no cooldown ≠ no re-cast), sustained damage and AD uptime are massively undermodeled for the remaining 165s. This is the single largest potential gap.

documentedConfounds.text: skill2: Dancing Flower Attack Damage ▲ 15.64%/15s is time-averaged to a permanent 1.30% (engine passive buffs cannot carry a duration) ⚑
documentedConfounds.why: Coupled to ⚑2: if S2 uptime is higher than 15/180, the AD buff is undermodeled. Even at kit-literal 15s the time-average is an engine-limitation approximation, not exact.

documentedConfounds.text: burst: 457.14% ×10 sequential attacks modeled as 10 same-frame hits vs the single boss; the stacking DoT assumes all 10 stacks apply per cast (351.6%/s ×10s) ⚑
documentedConfounds.why: Same-frame vs ~1-2s spread is negligible for total damage. Stack-application reading (⚑4) could reduce DoT to ×0.55 if per-second ramp, but that is a secondary effect on a 10s window every 40s.

documentedConfounds.text: cadence: datamined 12 pulls/s + 81-frame reload are unverified estimates ⚑
documentedConfounds.why: Cadence directly scales all normal-attack damage (the dominant source after t≈15). Unverified rate_of_fire / reload could shift NA DPS materially.

documentedConfounds.text: ⚑5 (low) sequential flavor tag on the nuke means teammates' Sequential Damage ▲ buffs scale it — text-derived.
documentedConfounds.why: Interaction-correctness note, not a gap in this unit's own damage. Only relevant in teams with Sequential Damage ▲ buffers.

authorIntentSummary: Parser-baseline HYPOTHESIS (not validated). The author models Sakura: Bloom in Summer as a one-shot S2 force-cast at t=0 (Sakura Petals 256%/s DoT for 15s + Dancing Flower AD time-averaged to 1.30% permanent) plus a burst nuke (457.14%×10 same-frame sequential hits) and a stacking burst DoT (351.6%/s×10s, all 10 stacks hit-applied per cast). All three part-destroy S1 triggers are deliberately skipped as inert vs the partless scope-lock boss. The top open question (⚑2) is whether S2 has any periodic re-cast — if so, both the DoT and the AD buff are massively undermodeled for the ~165s after the initial 15s window. Cadence (⚑1) and burst DoT stack timing (⚑4) are secondary unverified estimates. No board data exists (never fielded).
